decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed because it exceeded the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material is already at its maximum conductivity, the next most effective lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to improve lateral heat distribution. I will increase the spreader width from 0.84m to 1.0m (the maximum allowed bound) to maximize the area available for heat spreading towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader width increases the lateral conduction path to the Dirichlet boundaries (left/right at 25°C), reducing thermal resistance and lowering peak chip temperature. Material conductivity is already at its hard limit.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Increased heat flux towards the side boundaries
risk_notes: Spreader width is now at the upper bound (1.0m); future iterations cannot widen it further.
Ensure the spreader does not overlap with other components if the design changes later (currently valid as domain width is 1.2m).
Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or vertical transport.
